fields.name: Journal Template Name
fields.tooltip:
Shows the name of the journal template.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the General Journal Template table.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the journal line's entry number.
The program fills in the field automatically and uses the number to keep track of the various lines in the journal.

fields.name: Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of account that the entry on the journal line will be posted to.

fields.name: Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the account number that the entry on the journal line will be posted to.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date for the entry.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of document that the entry on the journal line is.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number for the journal line.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the entry.

fields.name: VAT %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the relevant VAT rate for the particular combination of VAT business posting group and VAT product posting group. Do not enter the percent sign, only the number. For example, if the VAT rate is 25 %, enter 25 in this field.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the general ledger, customer, vendor, or bank account that the balancing entry is posted to, such as a cash account for cash purchases.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the currency for the amounts on the journal line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total amount (including VAT) that the journal line consists of.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits.

fields.name: Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total amount in local currency (including VAT) that the journal line consists of.

fields.name: Balance (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the journal line's balance.
If the journal line contains only one entry, the balance will consist of this entry. If a balancing account has been entered on the journal line, the balance will be 0.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Sales/Purch. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line's net amount (the amount excluding VAT) if you are using this journal line for an invoice.

fields.name: Profit (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line's profit amount if you are using this journal line for an invoice.

fields.name: Inv. Discount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the invoice discount if you are using this journal line for an invoice.

fields.name: Bill-to/Pay-to No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the bill-to customer or pay-to vendor that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting group that will be used in posting the journal line.The field is used only if the account type is either customer or vendor.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Salespers./Purch. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the salesperson or purchaser who is linked to the journal line.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the source code that has been entered on the journal template.
The program automatically retrieves the source code from the Source Code field in the General Journal Template table.
The source code is automatically inserted on all journals that are created under the journal template.

fields.name: System-Created Entry
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: On Hold
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the journal line has been invoiced, and you execute the payment suggestions batch job, or you create a finance charge memo or reminder.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the due date on the entry.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

fields.name: Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment discount percent granted if payment is made on or before the date in the Pmt. Discount Date field.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items to be included on the journal line.

fields.name: VAT Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of VAT that is included in the total amount.

fields.name: VAT Posting
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

fields.name: Applies-to ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of entries that will be applied to when you choose the Apply Entries action.

fields.name: Business Unit Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the business unit that the project entry is linked to.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Shows the name of the journal.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the General Journal Template table.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Recurring Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring method if the Recurring field of the General Journal Template table indicates the journal is recurring.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date the recurring journal will be posted, if you have indicated in the journal is recurring.

fields.name: Recurring Frequency
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring frequency if the Recurring field of the General Journal Template table indicates the journal is recurring.

fields.name: Allocated Amt. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that has been allocated when you have used the Allocations function in the Gen. Jnl. Allocation table.

fields.name: Gen. Posting Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip:
Shows the VAT calculation type that the program will use when this entry is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Posting Setup table.
To see the available options, choose the field.


Option


Description


Normal VAT

With this option, the program calculates VAT for items on this line and creates a VAT ledger entry including the amount.


Reverse Charge VAT

This option is used when you trade with other countries/regions within the EU where the purchaser must compute and settle VAT accounts with the tax authorities.
Remember to also fill in the Reverse Chrg. VAT Acc. field.This option has no effect on your company's financial statements. When you sell goods with reverse charge VAT, you neither calculate nor withhold VAT. When you purchase goods, the program will calculate the VAT amount and debit the purchase VAT account and credit the reverse charge VAT account.


Full VAT

This option is used when the amount you want to post consists entirely of VAT. This option can be useful if, for example, you make a corrective entry in order to correct a VAT calculation error.


Sales Tax

This option is used if the program must calculate US sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: EU 3-Party Trade
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the entry was part of a 3-party trade. If it was, there is a check mark in the field.

fields.name: Allow Application
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the journal line can be applied to if you use the Balance recurring method.
If you want to allow application, insert a check mark in the field.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of account that a balancing entry is posted to, such as BANK for a cash account.

fields.name: Bal. Gen. Posting Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general posting type associated with the balancing account that will be used when you post the entry on the journal line.

fields.name: Bal. Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general business posting group code associated with the balancing account that will be used when you post the entry.

fields.name: Bal. Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general product posting group code associated with the balancing account that will be used when you post the entry.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip:
Shows the VAT calculation type that the program will use for the balancing account on this journal line when this entry is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Posting Setup table.
To see the available options, choose the field.
The following table describes the options.


Option


Description


Normal VAT

With this option, Microsoft Dynamics NAV the program calculates VAT for items on this line and creates a VAT ledger entry including the amount.


Reverse Charge VAT

This option is used when you trade with other countries/regions within the EU where the purchaser must compute and settle VAT accounts with the tax authorities.
Remember to also fill in the Reverse Chrg. VAT Acc. field. This option has no effect on your company's financial statements. When you sell goods with reverse charge VAT, you neither calculate nor withhold VAT. When you purchase goods, Microsoft Dynamics NAVwill calculate the VAT amount and debit the purchase VAT account and credit the reverse charge VAT account.


Full VAT

This option is used when the amount to be posted consists entirely of VAT. This option can be useful if, for example, you make a corrective entry in order to correct a VAT calculation error.


Sales Tax

This option is used if Microsoft Dynamics NAV must calculate US sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: Bal. VAT %
fields.tooltip:
Shows the Bal. VAT percentage that will be used on the journal line.
The program automatically retrieves the VAT percentage from the VAT Posting Setup table when the Bal. VAT Bus. Posting Group and Bal. VAT Prod. Posting Group fields are filled in.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of Bal. VAT included in the total amount.

fields.name: Bank Payment Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the payment type to be used for the entry on the journal line.

fields.name: VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: Shows the total net amount (amount excluding VAT) that the journal line consists of.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: Shows the total balance net amount (amount excluding VAT) that the journal line consists of.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry as a corrective entry. You can use the field if you need to post a corrective entry to an account.

fields.name: Check Printed
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether a check has been printed for the amount on the payment journal line.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip:
This field is automatically updated.

The field displays the type of ledger entry that is the source for this entry. If the field is blank, the source is a G/L account ledger entry.

fields.name: Source No.
fields.tooltip:
This field is automatically updated.

If the source for this entry is a customer, vendor, bank account or fixed asset ledger entry, the number in the field will be the number of the Customer, Vendor, Bank Account or Fixed Asset, respectively. If the source is a G/L entry, the field is empty.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series that will be used to assign a document number to the journal line when it is posted. To see the number series that have been set up in the No. Series table, choose the field.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the Posting No. Series field on the journal batch. You can change the code.
If this field is blank when you post the line, the document number in the Document No. field will also be used on the posted line.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer or vendor is liable for sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax group that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Use Tax
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the purchase is subject to use tax. Use tax is a sales tax that is paid on items that are purchased by a company and are used by that company instead of being sold to a customer.

fields.name: Bal. Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the tax area code group associated with the balancing account that will be used when you post the entry on the journal line.
To have sales tax calculated on the journal line, a check mark must be inserted in the Bal. Tax Liable field, and Bal. Gen. Posting Type must be Sale or Purchase.
The program will then use the tax area code, in combination with the Bal. Tax Group Code on the journal line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts to which sales tax will be posted.

fields.name: Bal. Tax Liable
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether to calculate sales tax on the balancing entry on the journal line.
To have sales tax calculated, you must also fill in Bal. Tax Area Code and Bal. Tax Group Code, and Bal. Gen. Posting Type must be Sale or Purchase.

fields.name: Bal. Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the tax group code associated with the balancing account that will be used when you post the entry on the journal line.
To have sales tax calculated on the journal line, a check mark must be inserted in the Bal. Tax Liable field, Bal. Gen. Posting Type must be Sale or Purchase.
The program will then use the tax group code, in combination with Bal. Tax Area Code on the journal line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts to which sales tax will be posted.

fields.name: Bal. Use Tax
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to calculate use tax for the balancing entry on this journal line.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved item or resource to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the VAT business posting group that will be used when you post the entry on the journal line.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the VAT product posting group that will be used when you post the entry on the journal line.

fields.name: Additional-Currency Posting
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: FA Add.-Currency Factor
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how to calculate the exchange rate if this field and the FA Add.-Currency Factor field for the FA posting types are specified in the Depreciation Book card window. This is the exchange rate that the program will use to record the transaction in the general ledger in both LCY and the additional reporting currency.
To see or change the exchange rate, choose the FA Add.-Currency Code field.

fields.name: Source Currency Code
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Currency Amount
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Curr. VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Curr. VAT Amount
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip: Indicates the percentage used for discounting the VAT element if a payment discount is to be posted. The value is validated against the maximum possible VAT Tolerance % field value in the General Ledger Setup. The maximum value cannot be overridden with the exception of a credit note where you can manually enter the value used for the payment discount.

fields.name: VAT Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount of VAT included in the total amount, expressed in LCY.
When the VAT Amount field is filled in, the program automatically calculates the VAT Amount (LCY) from the amount in the VAT Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
If the amount in the VAT Amount field changes, the amount in this field also changes accordingly.

fields.name: VAT Base Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total net amount (amount excluding VAT) that the journal line consists of, expressed in LCY.
When the VAT Base Amount field is filled in, the program automatically calculates the VAT Base Amount (LCY) from the amount in the VAT Base Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
If the amount in the VAT Base Amount field changes, the amount in this field also changes accordingly.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount of Bal. VAT included in the total amount, expressed in LCY.
When the Bal. VAT Amount field is filled in, the program automatically calculates the Bal. VAT Amount (LCY) from the amount in the Bal. VAT Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
If the amount in the Bal. VAT Amount field changes, the amount in this field also changes accordingly.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Base Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total balance net amount (amount excluding VAT) that the journal line consists of, expressed in LCY.
When the Bal. VAT Base Amount field is filled in, the program automatically calculates the Bal. VAT Base Amount (LCY) from the amount in the Bal. VAT Base Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
If the amount in the Bal. VAT Base Amount field changes, the amount in this field also changes accordingly.

fields.name: Reversing Entry
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Allow Zero-Amount Posting
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Ship-to/Order Address Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address code of the ship-to customer or order-from vendor that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the calculated VAT amount and a VAT amount that you have entered manually.

fields.name: Bal. VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the calculate VAT amount and the VAT amount that you have entered manually.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the code of the intercompany partner that the transaction is with if the journal line is an intercompany transaction. If your company created the line, this is the IC partner that you will send the transaction to. Otherwise, this is the IC partner that you received the line from.

fields.name: IC Direction
fields.tooltip: Indicates whether the intercompany line is one that you have created to send to one of your partner companies (outgoing), or a line that you have received from one of your partner companies (incoming).

fields.name: IC Partner G/L Acc. No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies outgoing intercompany transactions. It contains the IC G/L account that you suggest the amount will be posted to in your partner's company.
The field must be filled in on a line with a bank account or G/L account in either the Acc. No. field or the Bal. Account No. field.
If you have filled in the Default IC Partner G/L Acc. No field on the account card for the account in the Account No. field, the number has been automatically filled in by the program, but you can change it.

fields.name: IC Partner Transaction No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies incoming intercompany transactions. The program copies the transaction number from the Transaction No. field on the IC inbox journal line.

fields.name: Sell-to/Buy-from No.
fields.tooltip: There is no Help for the fields in this table. This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the company's VAT registration number.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a country/region code that applies to the journal line.

fields.name: Prepayment
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether prepayment is applied to the journal line.

fields.name: Financial Void
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Copy VAT Setup to Jnl. Lines
VAT Reporting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date used to include entries on VAT reports in a VAT period. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

fields.name: IC Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the account that you want to use for the transaction with your IC partner.

fields.name: IC Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the general ledger or bank account that the IC transaction is posted to.

fields.name: Job Queue Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of a job queue entry or task that handles the posting of general journals.

fields.name: Incoming Document Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the incoming document that this general journal line is created for.

fields.name: Creditor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor who sent the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Payment Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment of the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: Applies-to Ext. Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the external document number that will be exported in the payment file.

fields.name: Invoice Received Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was received.

fields.name: Recipient Bank Account
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account that the amount will be transferred to after it has been exported from the payment journal.

fields.name: Message to Recipient
fields.tooltip: Specifies the message exported to the payment file when you use the Export Payments to File function in the Payment Journal window.

fields.name: Exported to Payment File
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the payment journal line was exported to a payment file.

fields.name: Has Payment Export Error
fields.tooltip: Specifies that an error occurred when you used the Export Payments to File function in the Payment Journal window.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Remit-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address for the remit-to code.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project task.

fields.name: Job Unit Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit price, in the local currency, for the selected account type and account number on the journal line.

fields.name: Job Total Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total price for the journal line, in the local currency. The value is calculated as follows: Quantity x Unit Price (LCY).

fields.name: Job Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity for the project ledger entry that is derived from posting the journal line. If the Project Quantity is 0, the total amount on the project ledger entry will also be 0.

fields.name: Job Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project cost of one unit of the item or resource on the journal line, in the local currency. The value is calculated as follows: Project Total Cost (LCY) / Project Quantity.

fields.name: Job Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount percentage of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Line Disc. Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the amount of the discount that will be used for the job ledger entry.
The program uses the Job Line Discount %, Job Quantity, and Job Unit Price (LCY) fields to calculate the amount.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Job Unit Of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure code that is used to determine the unit price. This code specifies how the quantity is measured, for example, by the box or by the piece. The application retrieves this code from the corresponding item or resource card.

fields.name: Job Line Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of planning line to create when a project ledger entry is posted. If the field is empty, no planning lines are created.

fields.name: Job Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit price for the selected account type and account number on the journal line.

fields.name: Job Total Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total price for the journal line. The value is calculated as follows: Quantity x Unit Price (LCY).

fields.name: Job Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project cost of one unit of the item or resource on the journal line. The value is calculated as follows: Project Total Cost (LCY) / Project Quantity.

fields.name: Job Total Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you have assigned a project number and a project task number to the journal line. It shows the amount excluding VAT divided by the project quantity for the journal line. The amount is shown in the currency specified for the project. The value field is calculated as follows: (Amount - VAT Amount) x (Project Currency Rate/Currency Rate).

fields.name: Job Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount amount of the project ledger entry.

fields.name: Job Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line amount of the project ledger entry.

fields.name: Job Total Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project total cost if you have assigned a project number and a project task number to the journal line. It shows the Amount (LCY) excluding VAT Amount (LCY)for the journal line.

fields.name: Job Line Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line amount of the project ledger entry.

fields.name: Job Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Job Currency Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the currency code if the job specified on the journal line in the Job No. field is set up in a foreign currency.
The program uses the job currency code to calculate the cost and sales values of the journal line in the currency specified for the job.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Job Planning Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project planning line number that the usage should be linked to when the project journal is posted. You can only link to project planning lines that have the Apply Usage Link option enabled.

fields.name: Job Remaining Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to complete a project.

fields.name: Direct Debit Mandate ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the identification of the direct-debit mandate that is being used on the journal lines to process a direct debit collection.

fields.name: Data Exch. Entry No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Payer Information
fields.tooltip: Specifies payer information that is imported with the bank statement file.

fields.name: Transaction Information
fields.tooltip: Specifies transaction information that is imported with the bank statement file.

fields.name: Data Exch. Line No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Applied Automatically
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the general journal line has been automatically applied with a matching payment using the Apply Automatically function.

fields.name: Deferral Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the deferral template that governs how expenses or revenue are deferred to the different accounting periods when the expenses or revenue were incurred.

fields.name: Selected Alloc. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the allocation account number that will be used to distribute the amounts during the posting process.

fields.name: Campaign No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the campaign that the journal line is linked to.

fields.name: Prod. Order No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date of the related fixed asset transaction, such as a depreciation.

fields.name: FA Posting Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting type, if Account Type field contains Fixed Asset.

fields.name: Depreciation Book Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the depreciation book to which the line will be posted if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Type field for this line.

fields.name: Salvage Value
fields.tooltip: Specifies the estimated residual value of a fixed asset when it can no longer be used.

fields.name: No. of Depreciation Days
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of depreciation days if you have selected the Depreciation or Custom 1 option in the FA Posting Type field.

fields.name: Depr. until FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies if depreciation should be calculated until the FA posting date of the line.

fields.name: Depr. Acquisition Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies if, when this line was posted, the additional acquisition cost posted on the line was depreciated in proportion to the amount by which the fixed asset had already been depreciated.

fields.name: Maintenance Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a maintenance code.

fields.name: Insurance No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies an insurance code if you have selected the Acquisition Cost option in the FA Posting Type field.

fields.name: Budgeted FA No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a fixed asset with the Budgeted Asset check box selected. When you post the journal or document line, an additional entry is created for the budgeted fixed asset where the amount has the opposite sign.

fields.name: Duplicate in Depreciation Book
fields.tooltip: Specifies a depreciation book code if you want the journal line to be posted to that depreciation book, as well as to the depreciation book in the Depreciation Book Code field.

fields.name: Use Duplication List
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the line is to be posted to all depreciation books, using different journal batches and with a check mark in the Part of Duplication List field.

fields.name: FA Reclassification Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry was generated from a fixed asset reclassification journal.

fields.name: FA Error Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a posted FA ledger entry to mark as an error entry.

fields.name: Index Entry
fields.tooltip:
Displays if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Account Type field for this line.
Place a check mark in this field if you want to post an indexation.
The contents of this field will be copied to the corresponding field on FA ledger entries for information purposes only.
Note that you can also run the Index Fixed Assets batch job to index fixed assets.

fields.name: Source Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the source line number of the document from which the journal entry originates.

This field is used internally.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip: Specifies a comment about the activity on the journal line. Note that the comment is not carried forward to posted entries.

fields.name: Reverse Date Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies posting date calculation formula for reverse recurring methods.
